description: §23.5.4 — inheritance covers a nested foreign construct. The OUTER `<program>` declares `capabilities=[network("api.example.com")]`; the INNER `<program name="probe">` declares NO `capabilities=`, so it INHERITS the closest ancestor's set (the outer's non-empty `network`). The `use foreign:probe` construct is therefore governed by a NON-empty set → W-FOREIGN-UNDECLARED-CAPABILITY does NOT fire. The positive complement to the no-union case: when the inner does not re-declare, it inherits the outer's declaration.
rationale: §23.5.4: if a nested `<program>` does not declare `capabilities=`, the governing set is the CLOSEST ancestor that does — here the outer non-empty `network`. A foreign construct under a non-empty inherited set is declared, so the §23.5.5 presence-nudge stays silent. E-FOREIGN-SIDECAR-NOMINAL co-occurs (the §23.4 sidecar runtime is Nominal).
